trail.val: Low-NA EUV output ≥60 in 2026, scaling to ≥80 in 2027 (vs 44 in 2025)
MS raised 2027 EUV shipment forecast to 84 systems (from 80)
JPM: 90 EUV units 2027, 105 in 2028 (vs prior 81/85); capacity could do 110 Low-NA tools+ without new buildings

forward.note: 2027 unit confirmation above 80 the key buy-side trigger; capacity, not clean rooms, is the new framing.

trail.val: raised and narrowed FY26 to €36-40B, GM held 51-53%, H2-weighted

forward.note: Guide-raise driven by immersion + IBM upgrades, "nothing to do with EUV."

trail.val: UBS system+service WFE rev $30.0B(26E)→$34.6B(27E,+15%); blue-sky upside far above consensus
Redburn raised FY27-30 EPS +9-12%, sits 14% ahead of consensus; PT €1,900

forward.note: Street numbers seen as behind; buy-side already at €43-50 FY27 EPS so further upside needs capacity confirmation.

trail.val: non-EUV now expected to grow (vs prior flat) as immersion supply-chain bottleneck reversed
JPM models ~200 immersion units in 2027 (vs cons 137); immersion rev 34.8% above cons, total DUV 39.3% above

forward.note: Second under-modeled vector; China shells built 2026, equipped 2027.

trail.val: EUV layers/DRAM node 1b (~3-4) → 1c (~6-7, current) → 1d (~10, ~2028); DRAM materially stronger than CMD'24
TSMC booked 40-45 EUV for 2027, hard to get more as memory makers compete for same tools (DRAM EUV demand)

forward.note: DRAM is the new leg of the thesis; 2028 4F² transition a possible EUV-to-DRAM air-pocket (bear).

trail.val: CMD'24 frame reiterated: 2030 revenue €44-60B at 56-60% GM
UBS: WFE ~$250B CY28, into $300s CY29; ASML ~110 EUV slots ≈ ~$225B WFE before adding floor space
Redburn frames $68bn litho market / $54bn ASML system sales in 2030 at stable 91% litho share

forward.note: CMD expected early 2027 likely raises the 2030 €44-60B frame; BofA models €70B mid-case.
